title: Coffee Coded Days
body:
French Press mornings,
steam curling through a cracked window,
the skyline still half asleep.

Coffee too sweet to be coffee—
maybe that's how I survived New York.

Reseda Green trees lined the block,
their leaves catching the city's noise,
softening it,
like they knew I needed a pause.

Metropolitan gray followed me everywhere—
in the subway, in my reflection,
in the space between one dream and the next.

But I kept walking,
kept believing the taste would fade,
that maybe I'd learn to love the bitterness again.

And still—
every sip reminds me
how sweet survival can be.
